parameters.content:
## Calendly Meeting Prep Brief Generator

### How it works

1. The workflow triggers on a new Calendly email, filtering relevant events.
2. Extracts event details with AI and parses them for further actions.
3. Loads and matches building data from Google Sheets.
4. Searches and merges Gmail history to assess existing relationships.
5. Generates and formats brief emails based on available context and waits to send at scheduled times.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Configure Gmail API for the email trigger and sending nodes.
- [ ] Set up Google Sheets integration to access STR registration data.
- [ ] Obtain API keys for OpenAI to use its nodes.
- [ ] Configure permissions for HTTP requests to external APIs.

### Customization

Adjust email templates in the formatting nodes to customize the content for specific users or situations.

## Email trigger setup

Initiates workflow on new Calendly email events.

## Event detail extraction

Uses AI to extract and parse event details.

## CRM and lead detail search

Calculates send time and finds lead details in CRM.

## STR and Gmail data match

Matches building data to records and searches Gmail history.

## Email history processing

Processes email history to determine relationships or context.

## Email relationship summarization

Summarizes Gmail relationships with AI to build meeting briefs.

## Generate brief without history

Generates and parses meeting briefs when no email history exists.

## Timed meeting prep emails

Sends meeting preparation emails at the scheduled times.

## Research branch email handling

Handles scheduling and sending of research-focused emails.

parameters.subject: =Meeting Prep for {{ $('Find CRM Lead by Email').item.json.property_property_manager || $('Find CRM Lead by Email').item.json.property_managed_property || 'Upcoming Meeting' }}

parameters.jsCode:
const data = $json; // CHANGE NODE NAME if needed let buildingRaw = $('Derive Building Details via AI').first().json.text; buildingRaw = String(buildingRaw) .replace(/^text:/, "") .replace(/```json/g, "") .replace(/```/g, "") .trim(); const buildingData = JSON.parse($('Derive Building Details via AI').first().json.output[0].content[0].text) ; const bulletList = (arr) => (arr || []).map(item => `
  • ${item}
    • `).join(""); const htmlBody = `

    MEETING PREP

    QUICK SUMMARY

    ${data.building_research_summary}

    BUILDING

    • Address: ${$('Find CRM Lead by Email').first().json.property_managed_property}
    • Units: ${buildingData.units}
    • Stories: ${buildingData.stories}
    • Year Built: ${buildingData.year_built}
    • STR: ${buildingData.is_str ? "YES" : "NO"}
    • STR Matches: ${buildingData.str_match_count}

    RELATIONSHIP STATUS

    ${data.relationship_summary}

    INTEREST LEVEL

    ${data.interest_level}

    TOP PAIN POINTS

      ${bulletList(data.likely_pain_points)}

    IMPORTANT MEETING NOTES

      ${bulletList(data.meeting_prep_notes)}

    BEST TALKING POINTS

      ${bulletList(data.suggested_talking_points)}

    NEXT STEP

    Confirm decision-maker, current access workflow, and openness to a pilot deployment.

    `; return [{ json: { subject: `Meeting Prep - ${buildingData.address}`, body: htmlBody } }];

const data = $json; const bulletList = (arr) => (arr || []).map(item => `
  • ${item}
    • `).join(""); const htmlBody = `

    MEETING PREP

    QUICK SUMMARY

    ${data.quick_summary}

    BUILDING

    • Address: ${$('Find CRM Lead by Email').first().json.property_managed_property}
    • Units: ${data.building.units}
    • Stories: ${data.building.stories}
    • Year Built: ${data.building.year_built}
    • STR: ${data.building.is_str ? "YES" : "NO"}
    • STR Matches: ${data.building.str_match_count}

    RELATIONSHIP STATUS

    ${data.relationship_status}

    INTEREST LEVEL

    ${data.interest_level}

    TOP PAIN POINTS

      ${bulletList(data.top_pain_points)}

    IMPORTANT MEETING NOTES

      ${bulletList(data.important_meeting_notes)}

    BEST TALKING POINTS

      ${bulletList(data.best_talking_points)}

    NEXT STEP

    ${data.next_step}

    `; return [{ json: { subject: `Meeting Prep - ${data.building.address}`, body: htmlBody } }];

parameters.name: q
parameters.value: ={{ $('Find CRM Lead by Email').item.json.property_managed_property }} turnover rate floors units year built

values.content:
=Extract:
- stories
- units
- year built
- turnover rate

Return ONLY valid JSON.
for turnover rate write it out with 7 words max
Text:
{{ $json.ai_overview.text_blocks[0].snippet }}{{ $json.ai_overview.text_blocks[1].snippet }}

=Prepare a meeting brief.
Output ONLY in valid JSON
Use:

Email history:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.emailsToOrFromThem) }}

RETURN:
{
  "relationship_summary": "",
  "interest_level": "",
  "likely_pain_points": [],
  "meeting_prep_notes": [],
  "suggested_talking_points": []
}

Output must be under 150 words

=You are an AI sales meeting prep assistant.

Your job is to create a SHORT, highly scannable meeting prep brief for a sales rep before a meeting.

Keep responses concise and practical.
Do NOT write long paragraphs.
Use short sentences.
Prioritize actionable information.

If information is missing, say "Unknown".

GMAIL RELATIONSHIP SUMMARY:
{{ $json.output[0].content[0].text }}

BUILDING DATA:
{{ $('Derive Building Details via AI').item.json.output[0].content[0].text }}

STR CHECK:
{{ $('Align Building with STR Records').item.json.isSTR }}

NUMBER OF STR MATCHES:
{{ $('Align Building with STR Records').item.json.matchCount }}

STR CONTEXT:
If is_str = true or str_match_count > 0, treat this as a strong sales signal.

Why it matters:
- STR buildings likely have more guest turnover
- More key exchanges
- More temporary access needs
- More risk from physical lockboxes / shared keys
- Better fit for digital access systems, key management, and audit trails

When STR is true:
- Mention STR/key-exchange opportunity in quick_summary
- Include STR turnover/access as a pain point
- Include STR-specific talking points
- Make next_step focus on asking about guest/vendor/agent access workflows

Return ONLY valid JSON in this exact format:

{
  "quick_summary": "",
  "building": {
    "address": "",
    "units": "",
    "stories": "",
    "year_built": "",
    "is_str": false,
    "str_match_count": 0
  },
  "relationship_status": "",
  "interest_level": "",
  "top_pain_points": [],
  "important_meeting_notes": [],
  "best_talking_points": [],
  "next_step": ""
}

Rules:
- quick_summary = max 2 sentences
- relationship_status = max 1 sentence
- interest_level = one short phrase only
- pain points = short bullet-style phrases
- meeting notes = concise reminders only
- talking points = short tactical points only
- next_step = one sentence only
- no fluff
- no corporate wording
- no long explanations

parameters.subject: =Meeting Prep for {{ $('Find CRM Lead by Email').item.json.property_property_manager || $('Find CRM Lead by Email').item.json.property_managed_property || 'Upcoming Meeting' }}

values.content:
=You are researching a company, property, or building before a sales meeting.

Use the search results below to create useful meeting prep for selling an access/key-management solution.
Do not invent building-specific facts.
If search results do not clearly mention something, say "Unknown".
You may infer possible sales opportunities, but label them as possible, not confirmed.

Return ONLY valid JSON:

{
  "relationship_summary": "No prior email history found.",
  "building": {
    "address": "",
    "units": "",
    "stories": "",
    "year_built": "",
    "turnover_rate": "",
    "is_str": false,
    "str_match_count": 0
  },
  "interest_level": "Unknown",
  "likely_pain_points": [],
  "meeting_prep_notes": [],
  "suggested_talking_points": [],
  "building_research_summary": "",
  "useful_building_facts": [],
  "possible_opportunities": [],
  "risk_flags": []
}

Rules:
- Keep everything short and scannable.
- Do not use long paragraphs.
- Do not pretend there is email history.
- useful_building_facts must only contain facts found in search results.
- possible_opportunities can be sales assumptions based on building/property type.
- risk_flags can include unknowns like decision-maker, existing vendor, privacy concerns, or board approval.
- 250 words max.

SEARCH RESULTS:
{{ $json.ai_overview }}

BUILDING DATA:
{{ $('Derive Building Details via AI').item.json.output[0].content[0].text }}

STR CHECK:
{{ $('Align Building with STR Records').item.json.isSTR }}

NUMBER OF STR MATCHES:
{{ $('Align Building with STR Records').item.json.matchCount }}

=Extract the following information from this Calendly email.

Return ONLY valid JSON.

Fields:
- invitee_email
- event_date
- event_time

Rules:
- event_date format = YYYY-MM-DD
- event_time format = HH:MM PM/AM
- timezone = short readable timezone
- no markdown
- no explanations

EMAIL:

Subject:
{{ $json.Subject }}

Body:
{{ $('Verify Calendly Event Input').item.json.snippet }}

parameters.content:
## Building data processing

Fetches and processes building information from APIs.
